cards.title: First-Proof Benchmark Results
cards.direction: Treat proof search as navigation over an explicit ignorance partition: score every step on four gates in real time, log what is not yet known, and let the next move be discovered from the measurement rather than planned.

witnesses.headline: GPT-5.2 · 8 h unattended · ~$32 · 4 / 10 theorem-level proofs accepted · 85 steps audited (Harvard First Proof benchmark).
witnesses.note: The four gates mirror the Tetrad — compliance→Pl, inference→Coh, goal→Comp, robustness→Inv. The 29-point validity→robustness drop is the finding: the model rarely errs locally; it fails when a special case will not transport.

witnesses.alt: Problem-by-problem results across submissions: this work, OpenAI, Aletheia (Google), and the benchmark authors.
